change_comments.message: It all looks good except some minor comments.
Was this added to the REST interface in DMI?

inline_comments.message:
/**
 * Service interface to check the status of a data job.
 * The operations are implemented to interact with a DMI Plugin for data job status retrieval.
 */

/**
     * Retrieves the current status of a specific data job.
     *
     * @param dmiServiceName     The name of the DMI Service relevant to the data job.
     * @param requestId          The unique identifier for the overall data job request.
     * @param dataProducerJobId  The identifier of the data producer job within the DMI system.
     * @param dataProducerId     The ID of the producer registered by DMI, used for operations related to this request.
     *                           This could include alternate IDs or specific identifiers.
     * @return The current status of the data job as a String.
     */

/**
 * Implementation of {@link DataJobStatusService} interface.
 * This service retrieves the status of a data job by delegating to {@link DmiSubJobStatusRequestHandler}.
 */

NB: extract into method :

/**
     * Builds URL template parameters using DmiServiceUrlTemplateBuilder.
     *
     * @param dmiServiceName    Name of the relevant DMI Service.
     * @param requestId         Unique identifier for the outgoing request.
     * @param dataProducerJobId Identifier of the data producer job.
     * @param dataProducerId    ID of the data producer registered in the DMI service.
     * @return UrlTemplateParameters object with constructed URL parameters.
     */
    private UrlTemplateParameters buildUrlParameters(final String dmiServiceName, final String requestId,
                                                     final String dataProducerJobId, final String dataProducerId) {
        return DmiServiceUrlTemplateBuilder.newInstance()
                .fixedPathSegment("dataJob")
                .variablePathSegment("requestId", requestId)
                .fixedPathSegment("dataProducerJob")
                .variablePathSegment("dataProducerJobId", dataProducerJobId)
                .fixedPathSegment("status")
                .queryParameter("dataProducerId", dataProducerId)
                .createUrlTemplateParameters(dmiServiceName, dmiProperties.getDmiBasePath());
    }
Retrieves the status of a data job from the DMI service asynchronously.
The URL template parameters for the DMI data job status endpoint.
A Mono emitting the status of the data job as a String.
@throws DmiClientRequestException If there is an error during the DMI request.
Retrieves the status of a given data job identified by {@code requestId} and {@code dataProducerJobId}.
A ResponseEntity with HTTP status 200 (OK) and the data job's status as a string.
log.info("Received request to retrieve data job status. Request ID: {}, Data Producer Job ID: {}", requestId, dataProducerJobId);
done, but we dont put javadoc on private methods :)

Good question. We should not use Synchronously/Asynchronously word here as it returns Mono object and it depends on callee how one make use of mono object as it can be .block() for Synchronously and .subscribe(..) for Asynchronously.
This needs to be implemented in the stub in DMI Plugin now or it will be lost

NB: each on a new line
